children.text: I recently found an old photo of my school, and it looks completely different now.
children.cn: 我最近发现了一张我学校的老照片，它现在看起来完全不同了。
children.notes: completely: 完全地；different: 不同的
children.statement: completely 是副词，用来修饰形容词 different。

children.text: In the past, the school building was small and old, but today it is big and modern.
children.cn: 在过去，教学楼又小又旧，但今天它既大又现代。
children.statement: modern 意为“现代的”，是 old 的反义词。
children.explanation: 用来描述新建筑的特点。

children.text: We used to have only one small playground, but now we have two big ones for sports.
children.cn: 我们过去只有一个小操场，但现在我们有两个大的用于运动。
children.notes: used to: 过去常常；ones: 指代 playgrounds
children.statement: used to have 意为“过去曾有（现在没了）”。

children.text: The classrooms didn't have any computers or smart boards in the old days.
children.cn: 在以前的日子里，教室里没有任何电脑或智能白板。
children.notes: smart boards: 智能白板；in the old days: 在过去
children.statement: didn't have any 用于否定句表示“没有任何”。
children.explanation: any 通常用于否定句和疑问句。

children.text: However, every classroom is now full of high-tech equipment.
children.cn: 然而，现在每个教室都充满了高科技设备。
children.notes: however: 然而；be full of: 充满；equipment: 设备
children.statement: however 表示转折，通常用逗号隔开。

children.text: We also have a new library and a gym where we can enjoy our school life.
children.cn: 我们还有一个新的图书馆和一个体育馆，在那里我们可以享受我们的学校生活。
children.notes: gym: 体育馆；where: 在那里（引导从句）
children.statement: gym 是 gymnasium 的缩写。

children.text: I am absolutely happy with these amazing changes.
children.cn: 我对这些惊人的变化感到绝对开心。
children.notes: absolutely: 绝对地；amazing: 惊人的
children.statement: absolutely 是副词，意为“绝对地”。

children.text: I realized that our school is becoming much better and more modern every day.
children.cn: 我意识到我们的学校每天都变得更好、更现代。
children.explanation: 表示一种持续的变化过程。
